title: sericocarpus rigidus var. californicus
intro: Sericocarpus rigidus var. californicus, commonly known as California ironweed, is a perennial plant in the aster family, characterized by its tall stems and vibrant purple flowers. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of California ironweed are attributed to its ability to reduce fever, alleviate rheumatic pain, and support the immune system. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions and tinctures, which can be used to make teas, salves, and topical creams for various health applications.
